809.reason: Error: The error line number should be the line of the last non-terminal before the missing ')', which is the line of the StringConst token (children.get(2)), not the printf token (children.get(0)). This causes the error line number to be incorrect for error type j.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.2 , reason:  The error line number for missing ')' should be the line of the last non-terminal before the ')', which is the line of the last token in the printf arguments (e.g., the Exp or StringConst), not the line of 'printf'. This causes the wrong line number (19 instead of 19? Actually the expected is 19, but the buggy output shows no output, meaning the error is not detected or line number is wrong. The issue is that the error line is set to children.get(0).getLeaf().getLine() which is the line of 'printf', but the expected line is 19 (the line of the last token before ')', which is s[0] in this case). The correct line should be the line of the last token before the missing ')', which is the line of the last Exp or the StringConst. In the test case, the missing ')' is after s[0], so the line should be 19.

891.reason: The error line for missing ')' should be the line of the last non-terminal before the ')', which is the line of the last token in the Cond (or the '('). In the test case, the missing ')' is after 'f2() & f1()', so the line should be 18.

607.reason: [This loop condition is incorrect: it may exit before encountering '}', causing the parser to miss the closing brace. A better approach is to loop until a '}' token is seen or until end of tokens.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This loop condition is incorrect: it may exit before encountering '}', causing the parser to miss the closing brace. A better approach is to loop until a '}' token is seen or until end of tokens.]

618.reason: [If the loop exited because hasNext() became false without seeing '}', this line will attempt to fetch a token that does not exist, leading to an IndexOutOfBoundsException. The parser should instead report an error for missing '}'.]

1097.reason: Error: line number should be the line of the last token before the missing semicolon (e.g., the last ConstDef), not the first token. Per specification, "The error line number corresponds to the line where the last non-terminal before the semicolon is located."

Per specification, "The error line number corresponds to the line where the last non-terminal before the semicolon is located."

989.reason: If the loop breaks early (i < 3) because hasNext() is false, moving back by 3 can result in a negative index, causing an IndexOutOfBoundsException in subsequent lexer operations.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  If the loop breaks early (i < 3) because hasNext() is false, moving back by 3 can result in a negative index, causing an IndexOutOfBoundsException in subsequent lexer operations.

1013.reason: Similarly, if the loop breaks early (i < 2), moving back by 2 can result in a negative index, causing an IndexOutOfBoundsException.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Similarly, if the loop breaks early (i < 2), moving back by 2 can result in a negative index, causing an IndexOutOfBoundsException.

312.reason: The single '&' token is not added to the token list, causing the parser to skip it and potentially misinterpret the following tokens or crash due to unexpected syntax structure. It should be added as a token so the parser can detect the error or handle synchronization correctly.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  The single '&' token is not added to the token list, causing the parser to skip it and potentially misinterpret the following tokens or crash due to unexpected syntax structure. It should be added as a token so the parser can detect the error or handle synchronization correctly.

320.reason: The single '|' token is not added to the token list, causing similar issues as above.

873.reason: If getAndNext() consumed the last token, curIdx is tokens.size(). move(-2) makes it tokens.size()-2. If there was only 1 token left, this becomes -1, causing IndexOutOfBoundsException in the next line.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  If getAndNext() consumed the last token, curIdx is tokens.size(). move(-2) makes it tokens.size()-2. If there was only 1 token left, this becomes -1, causing IndexOutOfBoundsException in the next line.

874.reason: Accessing lexer.get() with an invalid index (potentially -1 or out of bounds) causes Runtime Error.
